root_text.an9.39:1.2: Tasmiṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, saṅgāme asurā jiniṁsu, devā parājayiṁsu.
root_text.an9.39:1.3: Parājitā ca, bhikkhave, devā apayiṁsuyeva uttarenābhimukhā, abhiyiṁsu asurā.
root_text.an9.39:1.7: Dutiyampi kho, bhikkhave, devā asurehi saṅgāmesuṁ.
root_text.an9.39:1.8: Dutiyampi kho, bhikkhave, asurāva jiniṁsu, devā parājayiṁsu.
root_text.an9.39:1.9: Parājitā ca, bhikkhave, devā apayiṁsuyeva uttarenābhimukhā, abhiyiṁsu asurā.
root_text.an9.39:2.4: Tatiyampi kho, bhikkhave, devā asurehi saṅgāmesuṁ.
root_text.an9.39:2.5: Tatiyampi kho, bhikkhave, asurāva jiniṁsu, devā parājayiṁsu.
root_text.an9.39:2.6: Parājitā ca, bhikkhave, devā bhītā devapuraṁyeva pavisiṁsu.
root_text.an9.39:2.8: ‘bhīruttānagatena kho dāni mayaṁ etarahi attanā viharāma akaraṇīyā asurehī’ti.
root_text.an9.39:2.10: ‘bhīruttānagatena kho dāni devā etarahi attanā viharanti akaraṇīyā amhehī’ti.
root_text.an9.39:3.2: Tasmiṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, saṅgāme devā jiniṁsu, asurā parājayiṁsu.
root_text.an9.39:3.3: Parājitā ca, bhikkhave, asurā apayiṁsuyeva dakkhiṇenābhimukhā, abhiyiṁsu devā.
root_text.an9.39:3.7: Dutiyampi kho, bhikkhave, asurā devehi saṅgāmesuṁ.
root_text.an9.39:3.8: Dutiyampi kho, bhikkhave, devā jiniṁsu, asurā parājayiṁsu.
root_text.an9.39:3.9: Parājitā ca, bhikkhave, asurā apayiṁsuyeva dakkhiṇenābhimukhā, abhiyiṁsu devā.
root_text.an9.39:4.4: Tatiyampi kho, bhikkhave, asurā devehi saṅgāmesuṁ.
root_text.an9.39:4.5: Tatiyampi kho, bhikkhave, devā jiniṁsu, asurā parājayiṁsu.
root_text.an9.39:4.6: Parājitā ca, bhikkhave, asurā bhītā asurapuraṁyeva pavisiṁsu.
root_text.an9.39:4.8: ‘bhīruttānagatena kho dāni mayaṁ etarahi attanā viharāma akaraṇīyā devehī’ti.
root_text.an9.39:4.10: ‘bhīruttānagatena kho dāni asurā etarahi attanā viharanti akaraṇīyā amhehī’ti.
root_text.an9.39:5.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, yasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati,
root_text.an9.39:5.2: tasmiṁ, bhikkhave, samaye bhikkhussa evaṁ hoti:
root_text.an9.39:5.3: ‘bhīruttānagatena kho dānāhaṁ etarahi attanā viharāmi akaraṇīyo mārassā’ti.
root_text.an9.39:5.5: ‘bhīruttānagatena kho dāni bhikkhu etarahi attanā viharati akaraṇīyo mayhan’ti.
root_text.an9.39:6.1: Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, samaye bhikkhu vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā …pe… dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ …
root_text.an9.39:6.4: tasmiṁ, bhikkhave, samaye bhikkhussa evaṁ hoti:
root_text.an9.39:6.5: ‘bhīruttānagatena kho dānāhaṁ etarahi attanā viharāmi akaraṇīyo mārassā’ti.
root_text.an9.39:6.7: ‘bhīruttānagatena kho dāni bhikkhu etarahi attanā viharati, akaraṇīyo mayhan’ti.
root_text.an9.39:7.1: Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, samaye bhikkhu sabbaso rūpasaññānaṁ samatikkamā paṭighasaññānaṁ atthaṅgamā nānattasaññānaṁ amanasikārā ‘ananto ākāso’ti ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.39:7.2: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ‘bhikkhu antamakāsi māraṁ, apadaṁ vadhitvā māracakkhuṁ adassanaṁ gato pāpimato tiṇṇo loke visattikan’ti.
root_text.an9.39:8.1: Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, samaye bhikkhu sabbaso ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma ‘anantaṁ viññāṇan’ti viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati …
root_text.an9.39:8.2: sabbaso viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma ‘natthi kiñcī’ti ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati …
root_text.an9.39:8.3: sabbaso ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati …
root_text.an9.39:8.4: sabbaso nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ upasampajja viharati, paññāya cassa disvā āsavā parikkhīṇā honti.
root_text.an9.39:8.5: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ‘bhikkhu antamakāsi māraṁ, apadaṁ vadhitvā māracakkhuṁ adassanaṁ gato pāpimato tiṇṇo loke visattikan’”ti.

translation_text.an9.39:0.3: The War Between the Gods and the Titans
translation_text.an9.39:1.1: “Once upon a time, mendicants, a battle was fought between the gods and the titans.
translation_text.an9.39:1.2: In that battle the titans won and the gods lost.
translation_text.an9.39:1.3: Defeated, the gods fled north with the titans in pursuit.
translation_text.an9.39:1.6: Why don’t we engage them in battle a second time?’
translation_text.an9.39:1.7: And so a second battle was fought between the gods and the titans.
translation_text.an9.39:1.8: And for a second time the titans won and the gods lost.
translation_text.an9.39:1.9: Defeated, the gods fled north with the titans in pursuit.
translation_text.an9.39:2.3: Why don’t we engage them in battle a third time?’
translation_text.an9.39:2.4: And so a third battle was fought between the gods and the titans.
translation_text.an9.39:2.5: And for a third time the titans won and the gods lost.
translation_text.an9.39:2.6: Defeated and terrified, the gods fled right into the castle of the gods.
translation_text.an9.39:2.7: When they had entered their castle, they thought,
translation_text.an9.39:2.8: ‘Now we’re in a secure location and the titans can’t do anything to us.’
translation_text.an9.39:2.10: ‘Now the gods are in a secure location and we can’t do anything to them.’
translation_text.an9.39:3.1: Once upon a time, a battle was fought between the gods and the titans.
translation_text.an9.39:3.2: In that battle the gods won and the titans lost.
translation_text.an9.39:3.3: Defeated, the titans fled south with the gods in pursuit.
translation_text.an9.39:3.6: Why don’t we engage them in battle a second time?’
translation_text.an9.39:3.7: And so a second battle was fought between the gods and the titans.
translation_text.an9.39:3.8: And for a second time the gods won and the titans lost.
translation_text.an9.39:3.9: Defeated, the titans fled south with the gods in pursuit.
translation_text.an9.39:4.3: Why don’t we engage them in battle a third time?’
translation_text.an9.39:4.4: And so a third battle was fought between the gods and the titans.
translation_text.an9.39:4.5: And for a third time the gods won and the titans lost.
translation_text.an9.39:4.6: Defeated and terrified, the titans fled right into the citadel of the titans.
translation_text.an9.39:4.7: When they had entered their citadel, they thought,
translation_text.an9.39:4.8: ‘Now we’re in a secure location and the gods can’t do anything to us.’
translation_text.an9.39:4.10: ‘Now the titans are in a secure location and we can’t do anything to them.’
translation_text.an9.39:5.1: In the same way, there’s a time when a mendicant, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, enters and remains in the first absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of seclusion, while placing the mind and keeping it connected.
translation_text.an9.39:5.2: At that time the mendicant thinks,
translation_text.an9.39:5.3: ‘Now I’m in a secure location and Māra can’t do anything to me.’
translation_text.an9.39:5.4: And Māra the Wicked also thinks,
translation_text.an9.39:5.5: ‘Now the mendicant is in a secure location and we can’t do anything to them.’
translation_text.an9.39:6.1: There’s a time when, as the placing of the mind and keeping it connected are stilled, a mendicant enters and remains in the second absorption …
translation_text.an9.39:6.4: At that time the mendicant thinks,
translation_text.an9.39:6.5: ‘Now I’m in a secure location and Māra can’t do anything to me.’
translation_text.an9.39:6.6: And Māra the Wicked also thinks,
translation_text.an9.39:6.7: ‘Now the mendicant is in a secure location and we can’t do anything to them.’
translation_text.an9.39:7.1: There’s a time when a mendicant, going totally beyond perceptions of form, with the disappearance of perceptions of impingement, not focusing on perceptions of diversity, aware that ‘space is infinite’, enters and remains in the dimension of infinite space.
translation_text.an9.39:7.2: This is called a mendicant who has blinded Māra, put out his eyes without a trace, and gone where the Wicked One cannot see.
translation_text.an9.39:8.1: There’s a time when a mendicant, going totally beyond the dimension of infinite space, aware that ‘consciousness is infinite’, enters and remains in the dimension of infinite consciousness. …
translation_text.an9.39:8.2: Going totally beyond the dimension of infinite consciousness, aware that ‘there is nothing at all’, they enter and remain in the dimension of nothingness. …
translation_text.an9.39:8.3: Going totally beyond the dimension of nothingness, they enter and remain in the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception. …
translation_text.an9.39:8.4: Going totally beyond the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception, they enter and remain in the cessation of perception and feeling. And, having seen with wisdom, their defilements come to an end.
translation_text.an9.39:8.5: This is called a mendicant who has blinded Māra, put out his eyes without a trace, and gone where the Wicked One cannot see. And they’ve crossed over clinging to the world.”

variant_text.an9.39:1.3: devā → devā bhītā (pts1ed) | apayiṁsuyeva → apayaṁsveva (bj); apassiṁsveva (sya-all); apayiṁsveva (pts1ed) | uttarenābhimukhā → uttarena mukhā (bj); uttarābhimukhā (sya-all) | abhiyiṁsu → abhiyaṁsu (bj); abhibhayiṁsu (sya-all)
